one_time_description: Subsequent to year-end, the First Nation received their share of the Robinson Huron Treaty settlement from the Government of Canada and Province of Ontario in the amount of $223,099,844. A motion was passed to distribute $178,500,000 to eligible members.
economic_notes: First Nation has a 20% ownership interest in N'Swakamok Forestry Corporation (NFC) which carries out forestry related activities. 100% owned Government Business Enterprise: Taighwenini Technical Services Corporation (TTSC), newly incorporated. Mining and mineral agreements with local mining companies including Vale Canada Limited, KGHM International, and Glencore. Ontario Power Generation settlement reserve of $1,559,657. Robinson Huron Treaty settlement of $223,099,844 received subsequent to year-end.
notes: Prior year (2023) revenue was $148,189,920 due to a $132,801,621 boundary settlement recorded as revenue, with a corresponding Trust Settlement expense of $127,372,330 and Contribution to Trust of $2,887,967, making prior year figures non-comparable. Subsequent to March 31, 2024, the First Nation received $223,099,844 from the Robinson Huron Treaty settlement and passed a motion to distribute $178,500,000 to eligible members - this is a very significant post-balance-sheet event. The GBE (TTSC) was newly incorporated during the year with only $100 contributed. No schedule of remuneration was included in the financial statements provided. Total government transfers calculated as Provincial ($619,005) + Federal ISC ($4,357,514) + CMHC ($266,139) = $5,242,658; Anishnawbek Nation, Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership, Kinoomaadziwin Education Body, and Union of Ontario Indians are treated as OSR as they are Indigenous organization transfers not direct government transfers. Current liabilities not separately disclosed in the text provided.
